requirements.label: Use Next.js latest stable runtime
Use Supabase official client libraries
Ship `.env.example` with required variables
requirements.gap: The plan includes `.env.example` variables but does not mention the required short comments for each variable.

requirements.label: Ignore `.env*` secrets except example
requirements.gap: The plan never commits to a `.gitignore` rule that excludes `.env*` secrets while preserving `.env.example`.

requirements.label: Configure build through env without code edits
Keep secrets out of repo and server-only
Provide app, test, reset command scripts
Include repeatable schema evolution artifacts
Use one stable namespace per build
Isolate namespaces and scope destructive resets
Attach every user record to `user_id`
Partition persisted data by namespace and user
requirements.source: infra_rider_prd.md > 4.3 Relationship between namespace and user

requirements.label: Support documented dev auth injection, prod-gated
requirements.source: infra_rider_prd.md > 5.1 Auth is not required to be "real" in benchmark mode
requirements.gap: The plan documents header/default-user injection but does not state how that mechanism is disabled or gated outside dev/test.

requirements.label: Real OAuth later needs no schema redesign
requirements.source: infra_rider_prd.md > 5.2 Migration to real OAuth must be straightforward

requirements.label: Keep backend as persisted source of truth
Make client cache safe to discard
Avoid Docker requirement for cloud-agent compatibility
requirements.gap: The plan assumes Supabase tooling but never says Docker is optional or unnecessary for cloud-agent execution.

requirements.label: Overlay saved user data on every show appearance
requirements.evidence: §5 Merge Logic; §9 Home Page; §9 Find Page
requirements.gap: The plan shows badges and merged detail views in several places but does not guarantee full My Data overlay on every show appearance, especially AI/search recommendation surfaces.

requirements.label: Support visible statuses plus hidden `Next`
requirements.gap: The plan has visible status chips but it collapses interest into status values and never models the hidden `Next` state.

requirements.label: Map Interested/Excited chips to Later interest
requirements.gap: `Excited` and `Interested` are treated as primary statuses instead of chips that map to `Later` plus an interest level.

requirements.label: Support free-form multi-tag personal tag library
Define collection membership by assigned status
requirements.gap: Home excludes items without status, but the plan never makes status the single definition of collection membership and even allows note-only saves without status.

requirements.label: Save shows from status, interest, rating, tagging
requirements.evidence: §5 Implicit Save Defaults; §9 Show Detail Page
requirements.gap: Status, rating, and tagging save paths are covered, but interest-triggered saves are folded into the wrong status model.

requirements.label: Default save to Later/Interested except rating-save Done
requirements.gap: The plan gets rating-to-Done correct but turns tag saves into an `interested` status instead of the required `Later` plus `Interested` defaults.

requirements.label: Removing status deletes show and all My Data
Re-add preserves My Data and refreshes public data
requirements.gap: Deleting the row on removal means the plan never preserves prior My Data for a later re-add or catalog refresh.

requirements.label: Track per-field My Data modification timestamps
requirements.gap: Several per-field timestamps exist, but the canonical interest timestamp and the full My Data timestamp set are not modeled.

requirements.label: Use timestamps for sorting, sync, freshness
requirements.gap: Timestamps are used for merge and Scoop freshness, but not for the broader sorting, sync, and freshness behaviors the PRD calls for.

requirements.label: Persist Scoop only for saved shows, 4h freshness
Keep Ask and Alchemy state session-only
Resolve AI recommendations to real selectable shows
requirements.source: product_prd.md > 5.8 AI Recommendations Map to Real Shows

requirements.label: Show collection and rating tile indicators
Sync libraries/settings consistently and merge duplicates
requirements.gap: Conflict-safe merges are mentioned, but duplicate detection and cross-device consistency behavior are not planned explicitly.

requirements.label: Preserve saved libraries across data-model upgrades
requirements.gap: Schema versioning is implied, but the plan never describes upgrade behavior that guarantees existing libraries survive model changes intact.

requirements.label: Persist synced settings, local settings, UI state
requirements.evidence: §2 user_settings and ui_state tables; §7 Settings & Utility
requirements.gap: Some settings and UI state are persisted, but the exact local settings called out in the PRD such as `autoSearch` and `fontSize` are not covered.

requirements.label: Keep provider IDs persisted and detail fetches transient
requirements.gap: The plan keeps provider data transient but does not persist provider IDs as opaque stored data for saved shows.

requirements.label: Merge catalog fields safely and maintain timestamps
Provide filters panel and main screen destinations
Keep Find/Discover in persistent primary navigation
requirements.gap: The plan defines a Find page but never specifies persistent primary navigation that keeps Find/Discover visible across the shell.

requirements.label: Keep Settings in persistent primary navigation
requirements.gap: The plan defines a Settings page but never specifies persistent primary navigation that keeps Settings visible across the shell.

requirements.label: Offer Search, Ask, Alchemy discover modes
Show only library items matching active filters
Group home into Active, Excited, Interested, Others
Support All, tag, genre, decade, score, media filters
requirements.source: product_prd.md > 4.5 Filters (Ways to View the Collection)
requirements.gap: Tag, genre, decade, and media filters are planned, but “All Shows” and community-score filtering are missing.

requirements.label: Render poster, title, and My Data badges
Provide empty-library and empty-filter states
Search by title or keywords
Use poster grid with collection markers
Auto-open Search when setting is enabled
requirements.gap: The plan never stores or honors an auto-open Search-on-launch setting.

requirements.label: Keep Search non-AI in tone
Preserve Show Detail narrative section order
requirements.gap: The planned page is close, but it does not explicitly preserve the canonical narrative hierarchy from the detail experience spec.

requirements.label: Prioritize motion-rich header with graceful fallback
requirements.gap: Backdrop/poster rendering is planned, but motion-prioritized trailer behavior with graceful fallback is not committed.

requirements.label: Surface year, runtime/seasons, and community score early
requirements.gap: Year and runtime/seasons are early, but the plan never explicitly brings community score forward with them.

requirements.label: Place status/interest controls in toolbar
requirements.gap: The toolbar placement is correct, but the control model is still status-based rather than a status-plus-interest relationship.

requirements.label: Auto-save unsaved tagged show as Later/Interested
requirements.gap: Tagging triggers an implicit save, but it saves as `interested` status instead of `Later` plus `Interested`.

requirements.label: Auto-save unsaved rated show as Done
Show overview early for fast scanning
Scoop shows correct states and progressive feedback
requirements.gap: Streaming and TTL are covered, but the required UI copy/state progression for no-scoop, cached-scoop, and open states is not.

requirements.label: Ask-about-show deep-link seeds Ask context
Include traditional recommendations strand
Explore Similar uses CTA-first concept flow
requirements.gap: Concept results are planned, but the CTA-first flow of “Get Concepts” then “Explore Shows” is not spelled out.

requirements.label: Include streaming availability and person-linking credits
Gate seasons to TV and financials to movies
Keep primary actions early and page not overwhelming
requirements.evidence: §9 Show Detail Page; §14 Key Design Decisions
requirements.gap: Primary actions are early, but the plan does not explicitly address the page-level busyness balance from the detail experience spec.

requirements.label: Provide conversational Ask chat interface
Answer directly with confident, spoiler-safe recommendations
requirements.gap: The plan says Ask is conversational, but it does not lock in direct-first, confident, spoiler-safe recommendation behavior.

requirements.label: Show horizontal mentioned-shows strip from chat
requirements.gap: The plan renders inline show pills in chat instead of the required horizontal mentioned-shows strip.

requirements.label: Open Detail from mentions or Search fallback
requirements.gap: Mentioned shows open Detail, but the required Search fallback for unresolved mentions is not planned.

requirements.label: Show six random starter prompts with refresh
requirements.gap: No starter-prompt welcome state or refresh behavior is specified for Ask.

requirements.label: Summarize older turns while preserving voice
requirements.gap: The plan stores chat history in component state but never summarizes older turns to preserve context economically.

requirements.label: Seed Ask-about-show sessions with show handoff
Emit `commentary` plus exact `showList` contract
requirements.gap: The plan uses custom `[[SHOW:id:title]]` tokens instead of the required structured `commentary` plus exact `showList` contract.

requirements.label: Retry malformed mention output once, then fallback
requirements.gap: No retry-once parser recovery path or fallback behavior is defined for malformed Ask mention output.

requirements.label: Redirect Ask back into TV/movie domain
requirements.gap: The plan references the AI prompting guide, but it never explicitly enforces redirecting off-domain queries back into TV/movie territory.

requirements.label: Treat concepts as taste ingredients, not genres
requirements.source: supporting_docs/concept_system.md > 1. What a Concept Is (User Definition)

requirements.label: Return bullet-only, 1-3 word, non-generic concepts
requirements.gap: The plan returns labeled concept objects with descriptions, which breaks the bullet-only, 1–3 word, no-explanation output contract.

requirements.label: Order concepts by strongest aha and varied axes
requirements.gap: The concept rules cite the source doc, but the plan never commits to strongest-first ordering or axis diversity.

requirements.label: Require concept selection and guide ingredient picking
requirements.gap: Concept selection is present, but the user-guidance copy about choosing ingredients is missing.

requirements.label: Return exactly five Explore Similar recommendations
requirements.gap: The plan never fixes Explore Similar to the required five recommendations per round.

requirements.label: Support full Alchemy loop with chaining
requirements.gap: The main loop exists, but the chaining step for continuing into another round is omitted.

requirements.label: Clear downstream results when inputs change
requirements.gap: The plan does not state that changing Alchemy inputs or concept selections clears downstream concepts/results.

requirements.label: Generate shared multi-show concepts with larger option pool
requirements.gap: Multi-show generation is covered generally, but the larger option pool requirement is not planned.

requirements.label: Cite selected concepts in concise recommendation reasons
requirements.gap: Recommendations include rationales, but those rationales are not required to cite the selected concepts explicitly.

requirements.label: Deliver surprising but defensible taste-aligned recommendations
requirements.gap: The plan mentions specificity, but it does not define the surprise-without-betrayal or taste-alignment quality bar for recs.

requirements.label: Keep one consistent AI persona across surfaces
Enforce shared AI guardrails across all surfaces
requirements.gap: The plan references the specs, but it does not establish one shared contract for spoiler safety, domain bounds, honesty, and actionability across all AI surfaces.

requirements.label: Make AI warm, joyful, and light in critique
requirements.gap: Warmth is present, but the full joy-forward and light-in-critique voice behavior is not explicitly preserved.

requirements.label: Structure Scoop as personal taste mini-review
requirements.gap: Scoop is personality-driven, but the plan does not require the full personal-taste mini-review structure and sections.

requirements.label: Keep Ask brisk and dialogue-like by default
requirements.gap: Ask is conversational, but the plan does not specify the brisk default length and dialogue-like pacing from the voice spec.

requirements.label: Feed AI the right surface-specific context inputs
requirements.gap: Show context and concepts are passed through, but the plan never makes library/My Data and other surface-specific context inputs universal where required.

requirements.label: Validate discovery with rubric and hard-fail integrity
requirements.gap: There is no planned rubric-based validation or hard-fail integrity check for discovery outputs.

requirements.label: Show person gallery, name, and bio
requirements.gap: Photo, name, and bio are covered, but the image gallery requirement is not.

requirements.label: Include ratings, genres, and projects-by-year analytics
requirements.gap: Ratings and genres are planned, but projects-by-year analytics are missing.

requirements.label: Group filmography by year
requirements.gap: Filmography is grouped by role instead of by year.

requirements.label: Open Show Detail from selected credit
Include font size and Search-on-launch settings
requirements.gap: The Settings plan omits both font-size and Search-on-launch settings.

requirements.label: Support username, model, and API-key settings safely
requirements.gap: Model and API-key settings are present, but username sync and the full safe-handling expectations are not fully specified.

requirements.label: Export saved shows and My Data as zip
Encode export dates using ISO-8601
requirements.gap: The export route is planned, but the ISO-8601 date-encoding requirement is not mentioned.

top_gaps.label: Map Interested/Excited chips to Later interest: The plan turns interest chips into standalone statuses, which distorts collection semantics, default saves, filters, and any downstream persistence logic tied to `Later`

Re-add preserves My Data and refreshes public data: The row-deletion approach means a user can lose prior tags, ratings, and Scoop history when they remove and later re-add a show, which is a direct durability regression

Emit `commentary` plus exact `showList` contract: Ask UI integration depends on this exact machine-readable contract, so the custom token format would break mentioned-show rendering and deterministic resolution

Default save to Later/Interested except rating-save Done: Because the implicit-save defaults are modeled incorrectly, common entry points like tagging produce the wrong relationship state and mis-shape the collection from first save onward

Enforce shared AI guardrails across all surfaces: Without a single shared AI contract, Ask, Scoop, Alchemy, and Explore Similar are likely to drift on spoilers, domain limits, honesty, and actionability, creating QA-visible inconsistency

narrative.overall_posture: The plan is structurally sound as an implementation blueprint, but it is not yet spec-tight. It covers the infrastructure baseline, page inventory, and broad feature set well, yet it leaves several fundamental product semantics only partially aligned, especially around collection state modeling and AI behavior contracts.
narrative.strength_clusters: The strongest coverage clusters are Benchmark Runtime & Isolation, Collection Home & Search, and the page-level shell for Show Detail and export. Those sections are concrete about Next.js/Supabase setup, namespace and user partitioning, migrations, API routes, home/search scaffolding, and the core screen inventory.
narrative.weakness_clusters: The biggest gaps cluster in Ask Chat, Concepts/Explore Similar/Alchemy, AI Voice/Persona/Quality, and Settings & Export, with an additional cross-cutting weakness in Collection Data & Persistence. The pattern is consistent: the plan usually names the feature, but it often stops short of the exact behavioral contracts, counts, fallback rules, and canonical data semantics that the PRD freezes.
narrative.risk_assessment: If executed as written, the first thing QA and stakeholders would notice is that the app feels broadly complete but behaves “off” in the highest-touch flows. Interest/status state would not match the product model, Ask would not emit the required mentioned-show contract or starter/summarization behaviors, and concept-driven discovery would feel thinner and less deterministic than the PRD requires.
narrative.remediation_guidance: The remaining work is mostly planning precision, not wholesale replanning. The plan needs a corrected canonical data model for status versus interest, explicit acceptance criteria for AI output contracts and fallbacks, exact UX/state rules for Ask and Explore Similar, and tighter settings/person-detail specifications so the implementation team is not left to infer benchmark-critical behavior.
